content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

November 6, 1995


Gerard A. Norve
6886 Serenity Court
San Jose, CA 95120

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-95-348

Dear Mr. Norve:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

Section 83114 and Regulation 18329 state that written advice
will be provided to any person who has duties under the Act or to
that person's authorized representative. Your questions are
general in nature and you have not indicated whether you are
seeking advice on your own behalf or as the representative of
another person who has duties under the Act. Therefore, we
decline to provide written advice to you.

However, enclosed is Regulation 18427, which sets out the
duties of treasurers and candidates under the campaign reporting
provisions of the Act. Also enclosed is a copy of a previously
issued advice letter which may assist you. (Davidson Advice
Letter, No. I-90-096.)


1/      Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Gerard A. Norve
Our File No. I-95-348
Page 2


If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Therefore, we decline to provide written advice to you. However, enclosed is Regulation 18427, which sets out the duties of treasurers and candidates under the campaign reporting provisions of the Act. Also enclosed is a copy of a previously issued advice letter which may assist you. (Davidson Advice Letter, No. 1-90-096.)
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked general questions about campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act, but did not clearly indicate whether they were seeking advice on their own behalf or as a representative of another person with duties under the Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the requestor's questions were general in nature and the requestor did not clearly establish that they have duties under the Act or are authorized to seek advice on behalf of someone who does. The FPPC instead provided regulatory guidance and a reference to a prior advice letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; the question and conclusion were extracted from the body text. The document follows a standard FPPC format declining written advice while offering alternative assistance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked general questions about campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act, but did not clearly indicate whether they were seeking advice on their own behalf or as a representative of another person with duties under the Act.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, we decline to provide written advice to you. However, enclosed is Regulation 18427, which sets out the duties of treasurers and candidates under the campaign reporting provisions of the Act. Also enclosed is a copy of a previously issued advice letter which may assist you. (Davidson Advice Letter, No. 1-90-096.)

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue a formal advice letter to Gerard A. Norve regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act because his questions were general and he did not establish whether he was seeking advice in his own capacity or as a representative of another person with duties under the Act. The FPPC provided regulatory materials and a prior advice letter as alternative assistance.
